title: web_fetch SSRF guard is IP-prefix string match — trivially bypassed by DNS rebinding and encodings
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

**Dimension:** Security and Access (SSRF / OWASP A10)

**Location:** /home/doll/OpenClaudia/src/web.rs lines 15-49 (validate_url)

**Violation:**
validate_url blocks private ranges via host.starts_with('172.16.') / '10.' / '192.168.' / etc. This is unsound:
1. **Hostnames bypass entirely.** 'http://internal-server.corp/' or 'http://metadata.google.internal/' do not match any IP prefix and pass validation. The check only runs on host_str() which for hostname URLs is the hostname, not a resolved IP.
2. **DNS rebinding.** An attacker-controlled domain whose DNS A record flips between public and 169.254.169.254 bypasses static checks; reqwest resolves at send-time and validate runs at parse-time.
3. **IPv6 coverage is incomplete.** Only [::1] is blocked. ::ffff:127.0.0.1 (IPv4-mapped), fc00::/7 (ULA), fe80::/10 (link-local), and 2002::/16 6to4 are all reachable.
4. **Decimal-encoded IPv4.** 'http://2130706433/' (= 127.0.0.1) parses as a valid URL with host '2130706433', matches none of the prefixes, and reqwest dials localhost.
5. **172.16/12 prefix is wrong.** 'host.starts_with("172.2")' (line 36) matches 172.2.0.0/16 through 172.29.0.0/16 BUT MISSES 172.20.*, 172.21.* etc being a substring check; also catches 172.200.x (public). 172.30-172.31 are public per RFC1918 (only 16-31 are private) — the code at line 37-38 BLOCKS public 172.30, 172.31.

**Mandated Refactor:**
1. Replace string-prefix checks with IpAddr::from_str parse of the resolved IPs. Use trust-dns-resolver or perform a socket DNS lookup; validate ALL returned addresses, not just one.
2. Use the ip_network / iprange crate with IANA special-purpose address registries: reject if any resolved IP is in private, loopback, link-local, multicast, benchmarking, documentation, reserved, or shared-address-space ranges.
3. Connect using a custom reqwest resolver that re-validates at dial-time — this closes the DNS-rebinding window.
4. Explicitly block cloud metadata services: 169.254.169.254 (AWS/GCP/Azure), fd00:ec2::254, metadata.google.internal, 100.100.100.200 (Alicloud).
5. Block hostnames that resolve to 0.0.0.0 or the unspecified IPv6 address.
6. Add a unit test for the decimal-encoded IPv4 bypass and for 'metadata.google.internal'.
